Title: R1 CoT Illegibility Revisited (nostalgebraist, fork of Jozdien/cot_legibility)
Summary: A LessWrong post/repo re-running DeepSeek-R1 GPQA CoT-legibility/correctness evaluations via OpenRouter, explicitly to test whether the original paper's Targon-served results generalize to other providers (Novita, Azure) — the post's core finding IS that OpenRouter provider choice changes R1's measured illegibility (2.30 mean on Novita vs 4.30 on Targon).
Importance/Impact: Author is nostalgebraist (creator of the logit lens, one of the most-cited independent alignment/interpretability writers on LessWrong/AI safety). Post directly engages a core AI-safety measurement (CoT faithfulness/legibility of DeepSeek-R1) and is itself a methodological correction of prior work (Jozdien's original cot_legibility results), making it influential as a cautionary case study for exactly the OpenRouter provider-routing risk this audit is scanning for.
What they use OpenRouter for: OpenRouter (openai-sdk-compatible client, base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1) is used to run inference on the open-weight model under study, DeepSeek-R1 (model_id "deepseek/deepseek-r1"), for the GPQA CoT-legibility and correctness evaluation whose statistics (mean illegibility, %>5, correctness %) are reported directly in the LessWrong post. Grading/judging (gpt-4o, claude-3-7-sonnet-latest) is done via direct OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, NOT via OpenRouter.
Why this safety class: Uses OpenRouter on a critical route and controls for it: pins extra_body['provider']={'only':['novita'],'allow_fallbacks':False}, logs the served provider per call (verified in the run artifacts), and runs the grader on direct OpenAI/Anthropic APIs so no judge route can be corrupted. Provider routing is itself the study's research question.
Headline possibly-impacted claim: None on the specific reported numbers, but the first-pass agent's evidence trail was wrong on two points that needed correcting. Independently re-derived straight from raw inference.json: Novita mean=2.280, correct_pct=40.5% (n=300, run runs/20260419_103551_R1_gpqa, config config/2026_04_19__r1__all__novita_only__100q_3rep.yaml — 100 GPQA questions x 3 samples, matching the post's own stated sample config; 300/300 records' metadata.openrouter_provider == "Novita"). Targon mean=4.305, correct_pct=36.6%, Illeg>=5=34.9% (103/295) all match exactly in run runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa; 295/300 records' metadata.openrouter_provider == "Targon" (the other 5 are request-level failures with Targon-attributed 429s), 0 Nebius — corrected 2026-07-21 against the raw inference.json, see findings/observed_routing.json record r1-targon-allowlist-a. The Novita "Illeg>=5: 0.5%" table cell does NOT reproduce from that same 300-response run (which gives 1.0% = 3/300); it instead matches a different, smaller 198-response/200-question Novita run (runs/20260419_100716_R1_gpqa, 1/198=0.505%) that the first-pass agent cited as if it backed the whole headline table. That run is also independently confirmed 100% Novita-provenance, so the OR-safety conclusion is unaffected either way, but the citation was imprecise (wrong run for the mean/correctness cells; the post's own table may itself be blending two different runs' cells, which is a data-integrity question for the author, not an OpenRouter-routing one). Separately, the first-pass agent's claim that the Targon numbers come from "archived rollouts...with no inference stage" is factually wrong (see added finding) — but this doesn't change the SAFE verdict, since the actual live-generated Targon data is independently confirmed single-provider.
Possibly-impacted findings (specific fig:
[streamlit_runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/config.yaml#L13-15 (original inference config; runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/config.yaml was overwritten in-place by a later analysis-only re-run and no longer shows the inference-stage config); runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/inference.json (per-record metadata.openrouter_provider, verified 300/300='Targon'); src/inference/providers.py#L36-41] The Targon-side baseline run backing the headline 4.305 mean-illegibility / 36.6% correctness / 34.9% Illeg>=5 figures was NOT sourced from Jozdien's archived rollouts as the first-pass agent claimed (no archive/ directory exists anywhere in the repo, and this run's metadata schema — populated duration_ms/tokens/provider_model/openrouter_provider — matches a live API call, not scripts/convert_archived_rollouts.py's output format, which instead writes {source:'archive_conversion', original_file, duration_ms:0} and never touches OpenRouter at all). It is a live, this-repo-generated OpenRouter call using an UNPINNED TWO-PROVIDER allow-list (openrouter_provider: [targon/fp8, Nebius], allow_fallbacks:False) — not a single pinned provider like the Novita arm. Empirically all 300/300 logged metadata.openrouter_provider values read 'Targon' (0 Nebius), so the reported Targon numbers are not actually contaminated. But this was an empirical outcome, not a structural guarantee: this same repo demonstrably DOES silently mix OpenRouter backends within a nominally-pinned allow-list elsewhere in its own committed run history (every qwq_gpqa run under streamlit_runs/ mixes DeepInfra+Nebius within a single run; one Kimi-K2 run mixes Moonshot AI+Novita; one R1-Distill-Qwen-32B run mixes NextBit+Novita), and the post's own narrative independently confirms this same R1/GPQA experiment DID mix providers (Novita+Azure) in an earlier, provider-unpinned preliminary run that was discarded specifically because Azure responses frequently truncated mid-CoT — so the 'no mixing occurred' result for the reported Targon arm is a lucky empirical finding that a re-run of the identical config is not guaranteed to reproduce, not something the config itself enforced the way the Novita arm's true single-item pin does.
    → mechanism: M3 (probabilistic provider routing within an `only` allow-list of 2 providers rather than a true single pin) feeding one arm of what is otherwise correctly framed as an intentional M8-style cross-provider comparison
    → severity: low | depends-on-OpenRouter: medium
Why nothing impacted:
Re-verified against the actual repo (branch `nost`) rather than trusting the first pass:

1. Pin mechanism confirmed at src/inference/providers.py L36-41: a list-valued `openrouter_provider` in model_config becomes `extra_body["provider"] = {"only": provider_config, "allow_fallbacks": False}`. Matches the task's cited primary call site.

2. Novita headline cells (mean 2.280, std 0.754, Illeg>=3 33%, Illeg>=7 0%, correct_pct 40.5%) all reproduce exactly, bit-for-bit, from runs/20260419_103551_R1_gpqa/evaluation.json — the run generated by config/2026_04_19__r1__all__novita_only__100q_3rep.yaml (100 GPQA questions x 3 samples = 300 responses, openrouter_provider:[novita]), matching the post's own stated "first 100 questions..with 3 samples drawn per run." I independently confirmed 300/300 records' metadata.openrouter_provider == "Novita", metadata.provider_model == "deepseek/deepseek-r1". This is a DIFFERENT run than the one the first-pass agent verified (it checked runs/20260419_100716_R1_gpqa, a 198-response/200-question single-sample run, which is also 198/198 Novita-confirmed but only backs the post's informal "~2.30" narrative aside and, oddly, the "Illeg>=5: 0.5%" table cell (1/198=0.505%) rather than the mean/correctness cells — the 300-run itself gives 1.0% (3/300) for that threshold, not 0.5%). Net effect: both candidate Novita runs are independently confirmed single-provider, so this discrepancy is a citation-precision issue, not an OpenRouter-safety issue.

3. Targon headline cells (mean 4.305, Illeg>=3 79.3%, Illeg>=7 20.3%, Illeg>=5 34.9%, correct_pct 36.6%) reproduce exactly from runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/evaluation.json. Contrary to the first-pass agent's claim, this run is NOT from archived markdown rollouts (no archive/ dir exists in the repo; the metadata schema is the live-call schema, not convert_archived_rollouts.py's output schema) — it is a live OpenRouter call using an unpinned 2-provider allow-list (see added finding). Empirically confirmed 300/300 = "Targon", 0 "Nebius", so the reported numbers are not corrupted, just less rigorously controlled than claimed.

4. M9 (judge on unconstrained route): confirmed not applicable. src/utils/models.py L2 maps "gpt-4o" to {"provider":"openai", ...}; get_provider("openai") returns DirectAPIProvider using OPENAI_API_KEY via the OpenAI SDK directly — never touches openrouter.ai. Grader temperature is hardcoded to 0.0 in src/evaluation/grader.py regardless of OR concerns, but the grader is off OpenRouter entirely either way.

5. M1 (quantization): the post states it externally verified fp8 for both providers; the Targon config explicitly requests "targon/fp8" while the Novita config only requests bare "novita" (no quant suffix) — a genuine minor asymmetry (Novita's quant isn't config-enforced), but not large enough to plausibly produce a 0.5-1.0pp vs 34.9pp illegibility gap.

6. Positive corroborating evidence for the SAFE verdict: the post's own prose documents that the author actually encountered real OpenRouter multi-provider mixing (Novita+Azure) in an earlier, provider-unpinned preliminary run for this exact study, noticed the resulting data-quality problem (Azure truncating CoT, producing empty final answers), and discarded that run in favor of a pinned, single-provider Novita re-run — i.e., the exact failure mode this audit exists to catch was independently caught and corrected by the author before the reported headline numbers were computed.

Given the explicit Novita pin (config + empirically-verified 100% single-provider logs on the correct run), the empirically-clean (if not config-guaranteed) Targon logs, and a judge model that bypasses OpenRouter entirely, none of the three headline metric pairs (mean illegibility, %>=5, correctness) plausibly route through an OpenRouter mistake pathway for THIS reported comparison. This is consistent with the pre-assigned SAFE verdict, but the "Targon = archived, uncontrolled-Novita-risk-only" framing in the first-pass reasoning was incorrect and has been corrected above.
Safeguards present: order/only provider pinning (extra_body['provider']={'only':['novita'],'allow_fallbacks':False}), provenance logging of served provider and provider_model per-call, verified empirically in output data, grader/judge runs on direct OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, not OpenRouter, so no judge route to corrupt, provider-routing effect made the explicit subject of the study and disclosed at length in the post
Provider A/B rerun assessment: HIGHEST-VALUE EVIDENCE, AND IT IS ALREADY FREE. This is the repo our survey marked SAFE, and its committed runs/ directory already contains the natural experiment: same slug deepseek/deepseek-r1, same GPQA-diamond pipeline, provenance logged per response (src/inference/providers.py:104). Unpinned runs served mostly by Targon score mean illegibility 4.31 +/- 2.13 (n=295) at 36.6% accuracy; provider-pinned Novita runs score 2.31 +/- 0.75 (n=198) at 43.9% — an ~88% swing in the headline metric under one model name. One run under the two-entry allow-list returned only 169/300 responses from Targon; corrected 2026-07-21 — not drift to another backend (Nebius served zero of the 600 requests across both allow-list runs) but 125 Targon 429s plus 6 parse/None errors becoming missing data because allow_fallbacks was false, see findings/observed_routing.json record r1-targon-allowlist-b and landed at 31.4% accuracy, a 12.5-point spread against a pinned run. Caveat: those runs are 6 months apart, so checkpoint drift is confounded with provider — which is precisely the argument for the paired same-day rerun. Rerunning is zero-config (`openrouter_provider` is a first-class YAML field; the author pins in endpoint-tag form e.g. `targon/fp8`, `deepinfra/bf16`, confirming provider.only accepts slug/quant). Weakness: deepseek/deepseek-r1 now has only 2 live endpoints — azure (ctx 163840, $1.485/$5.94) vs novita/fp8 (ctx 64000, $0.70/$2.50) — a modest, partly-unknown quality gap. ~$15 and ~1 h if rerun; $0 to cite the existing numbers.
Signs provider issues messed up the pape: In nostalgebraist's cot_legibility repo, streamlit_runs/20251014_190506_R1_gpqa/{config.yaml,inference.json,evaluation.json} (commit 89195c70, config pinned to openrouter_provider: [targon/fp8, Nebius]) hold 300 raw per-row DeepSeek-R1 GPQA generations, and a later rerun on branch nost (f4437e46) at runs/20260419_103551_R1_gpqa/inference.json repeats the identical 100Q x 3-sample design pinned to openrouter_provider: [novita]. 295 of 300 Targon rows carry metadata.openrouter_provider=="Targon" (300/300 for Novita), and evaluation.json's statistics block gives a legibility mean of 4.305084745762712 over count 295, with correctness correct_pct of 36.6. The gpqa_98/sample1 garbled-reasoning quote appears byte-for-byte in the raw reasoning field around chars 43100-44300, and it is genuine multi-language token degeneration rather than mojibake — the inserted foreign words are semantically apt (e.g., 碳 = "carbon" mid-chemistry-reasoning) — consistent with corrupted-but-real sampling rather than a UTF-8/streaming decode bug, since the harness's OpenRouterProvider (src/inference/providers.py) uses the OpenAI SDK's standard SSE stream parsing with clean provider pinning via extra_body["provider"]={"only":[...],"allow_fallbacks":False} and no ad hoc byte handling that could manufacture corruption. Counting reasoning traces that fuse 3+ distinct non-Latin scripts (CJK/Cyrillic/Hangul/Hebrew/Arabic/Bengali/Armenian/Georgian/etc.) within single malformed sentences gives 63/295 (21%) of Targon rows against 0/300 Novita rows — a systematic, provider-correlated pattern rather than an isolated anecdote. Novita's own highest-non-ASCII rows are ordinary math notation or clean single-language Chinese reasoning switches, the documented non-fingerprint R1 behavior, qualitatively distinct from Targon's cross-script fusion. Of the 5 rows Targon excludes from the n=295 statistics, 4 are HTTP 429 rate-limit errors and the fifth (gpqa_45/sample0) is a JSON-decode failure ("Expecting value: line 1531 column 1") rather than a rate limit, though it remains a legitimate exclusion either way. This compares one run per provider six months apart, so model-version drift in OpenRouter's routing — not quantization per se — cannot be fully excluded as a contributor, though the config's explicit "targon/fp8" tag targets exactly the mechanism under investigation.
